name: Horus
shortDescription: Horus is an ancient Egyptian god of the sky, kingship, and protection. Depicted as a falcon or a man with a falcon head, he is central to Egyptian mythology.
description: Horus is one of the most significant deities in ancient Egyptian religion, worshipped from at least the late Predynastic period through to Greco-Roman times. He was often represented as a falcon, most likely a Lanner falcon or Peregrine falcon, or as a man with a falcon head. The most prevalent myth concerning Horus details his birth and childhood following the murder of his father, Osiris, by his uncle Set. Isis, Horus's mother, hid him in the marshes of the Nile Delta to protect him from Set. Horus eventually avenged his father by battling Set, a conflict that resulted in Horus losing an eye (the Eye of Horus, a powerful symbol of protection, health, and restoration). After winning the battle, Horus became the rightful ruler of Egypt, embodying the divine kingship. His role as a sky god is also significant, with his eyes often associated with the sun and moon. While the context of the provided mythology is listed as Jewish, it is important to note that Horus originates from Egyptian mythology. The reference within a Jewish context is likely related to comparative mythology and potential interpretations or influences across different cultures, rather than a direct integration or belief within Judaism itself.
